content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /agent/data/agent-workflow/.claude/skills/respond-to-review

# Respond to Review

Addresses all open review comments on the implementation PR — inline review
threads (resolved via GraphQL) and general PR comments (acknowledged with a
marker reply) — then pushes changes, converts the PR back to ready-for-review,
and records a `comments_addressed` log entry in the task YAML.

## Context

All required values are provided in the agent context under **## Your claimed task**:

| Key | Description |
|---|---|
| `Workspace root` | Absolute path to the management (workspace) repo |
| `Feature` | Feature ID (e.g. `agent-pr-response`) |
| `Task ID` | Task ID (e.g. `T4`) |
| `Impl repo` | Implementation repo ID |
| `Repo root` | Absolute path to the implementation repo |
| `Branch` | Feature branch name (e.g. `feature/agent-pr-response-T4`) |
| `PR URL` | GitHub PR URL (`https://github.com/{owner}/{repo}/pull/{number}`) |

The `GITHUB_TOKEN` environment variable is set for all GitHub API calls.

---

## Execution Steps

### Step 1 — Parse the PR URL

Extract `owner`, `repo`, and `pull_number` from the `PR URL`:

```
https://github.com/{owner}/{repo}/pull/{pull_number}
```

### Step 2 — Set PR to Draft

Signal that the agent is working on this PR:

```bash
curl -s -X PATCH \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" \
  -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
  "https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}" \
  -d '{"draft": true}'
```

If the PR is already a draft or the call fails, continue — this is a best-effort signal.

### Step 3 — Fetch Unresolved Inline Review Threads via GraphQL

```bash
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://api.github.com/graphql" \
  -d '{
    "query": "query($owner:String!,$name:String!,$number:Int!){repository(owner:$owner,name:$name){pullRequest(number:$number){reviewThreads(first:100){nodes{id isResolved comments(first:10){nodes{databaseId body author{login}}}}}}}}",
    "variables": {"owner": "{owner}", "name": "{repo}", "number": {pull_number}}
  }'
```

From the response, build one list:

**Unresolved inline threads** (`reviewThreads.nodes` where `isResolved: false`):
- Each thread has an `id` (node ID) needed for resolving
- The thread's first comment describes the requested change

General PR-level issue comments are not tracked — only inline review threads are addressed.

If the list is empty, skip to Step 7.

### Step 4 — Check Out the Implementation Branch

```bash
cd <Repo root>
git fetch origin
git checkout <Branch>
git reset --hard origin/<Branch>
```

### Step 5 — Address Each Comment

For each unresolved inline thread:
1. Read the thread's first comment to understand the requested change.
2. Edit the referenced file and line.
3. Stage and commit:
   ```bash
   git add <changed-files>
   git commit -m "fix: address review comment — <brief description>"
   ```

Batch closely related changes into a single commit. Unrelated changes must be
separate commits. After addressing all comments, run any available type-check or
lint commands to confirm no regressions (e.g. `npm run typecheck` or `tsc --noEmit`).

### Step 6 — Push Changes

```bash
git push origin <Branch>
```

Stop and report if the push is rejected. Do not update the task YAML.

### Step 7 — Resolve Each Inline Thread

For each thread that was addressed, resolve it via GraphQL:

```bash
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://api.github.com/graphql" \
  -d '{"query":"mutation($threadId:ID!){resolveReviewThread(input:{threadId:$threadId}){thread{isResolved}}}","variables":{"threadId":"{thread.id}"}}'
```

### Step 8 — Convert PR to Ready-for-Review

```bash
curl -s -X PATCH \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" \
  -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
  "https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}" \
  -d '{"draft": false}'
```

Verify the response contains `"draft": false`. If the API returns an error,
log the details and continue — the task YAML must still be updated.

### Step 9 — Update the Task YAML in the Management Repo

Read the task YAML:
```
<Workspace root>/docs/features/<Feature>/tasks/<Task ID>.yaml
```

Append to the `log` array:
```yaml
- action: comments_addressed
  by: <GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL>
  at: <ISO-8601 timestamp with timezone>
  note: "Resolved N inline review thread(s); PR converted to ready-for-review."
```

Also update:
- `execution.last_updated_by` → `GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL`
- `execution.last_updated_at` → current ISO-8601 timestamp

Write the updated YAML back to the same path.

### Step 10 — Commit and Push the Task YAML

In the management repo:
```bash
cd <Workspace root>
git fetch origin
git checkout <Branch>
git reset --hard origin/<Branch>
git add docs/features/<Feature>/tasks/<Task ID>.yaml
git commit -m "chore(<Task ID>): comments_addressed — PR converted to ready-for-review"
git push origin <Branch>
```

---

## No-comment fast path

If Step 3 finds no unresolved inline threads:
- Skip Steps 4–7
- Still perform Step 8 (ensure PR is ready-for-review — it may have been left in draft)
- Write the log entry with note "No unresolved threads found; PR confirmed ready-for-review"

---

## Error Handling

| Situation | Action |
|---|---|
| GraphQL query fails | Stop; report the error. Do not attempt to address anything. |
| `git push` rejected on impl branch | Stop; report the error. Do not update task YAML. |
| `resolveReviewThread` mutation fails | Log the error in the `comments_addressed` note; continue with remaining threads |
| PR already not in draft state (Step 8) | Skip Step 8; still write `comments_addressed` log entry |
| Task YAML parse error | Stop and report — do not write malformed YAML |

---

## Hard Stop Rule — Missing Tools

If any shell command exits with code 127 ("command not found"), do NOT attempt
to install it or work around it. Stop immediately and report which tool is missing.

# Agent-runtime — local multi-agent compose
#
# Two long-running agent services. Each runs a continuous internal polling loop,
# pulling workspace state and scanning for ready tasks on every cycle.
# Agent-2 is commented out by default; uncomment to run two agents in parallel.
#
# restart: unless-stopped provides crash recovery only — the poll cadence is
# controlled by idle_sleep_seconds in agent.yaml (default: 60 s), not by
# container restarts.
#
# RAG stack (qdrant + rag-server + indexer) is optional — agents degrade
# gracefully when MCP_RAG_URL is unset. To enable:
#   1. Set RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH in .env to the path of your local rag-service clone.
#   2. Set WORKSPACE_ID in .env (e.g. "workspace").
#   3. Set REPO_PATHS in .env to comma-separated paths of repos to index
#      (e.g. "/agent/data,/agent/workflow").
#   4. docker compose --profile rag up --build
#
# Usage (from this directory):
#   cp agent.yaml.example agent.yaml   # fill in watches: with your workspace SSH URL
#   cp .env.example .env               # fill in required values
#   docker compose up --build          # start agents only (no RAG)
#   docker compose --profile rag up --build  # start agents + RAG stack
#   docker compose up --build -d       # start in background

name: agent-runtime-local

# ── Shared agent definition ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
x-agent: &agent-base
  build:
    # Build context is workflow/ root so schemas/ is included.
    # Path is three levels up: local/ → orchestration/ → agent-runtime/ → workflow/
    context: ../../..
    dockerfile: agent-runtime/Dockerfile
  env_file: .env
  environment:
    AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
    WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
    WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
    # Required for the pr-create skill (GitHub REST API).
    GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
    # SSH key delivery: SSH_PRIVATE_KEY (env var, preferred) or SSH_KEY_PATH (file mount).
    SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
    SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
    # RAG context injection — optional. When set, the agent queries the RAG server
    # for relevant project context before starting each task.
    # Empty (default) = graceful cold start — no rag_query calls attempted.
    # Set in .env: MCP_RAG_URL=http://rag-server:8000 (when --profile rag is used).
    MCP_RAG_URL: "${MCP_RAG_URL:-}"
  volumes:
    - ./agent.yaml:/agent/agent.yaml:ro
    - workspaces:/agent/data
    - workflow:/agent/workflow
    - ${SSH_KEY_DIR:-${HOME}/.ssh}:/agent/ssh:ro
  restart: unless-stopped
  deploy:
    resources:
      limits:
        cpus: "4"
        memory: 10G
      reservations:
        cpus: "1"
        memory: 2G

# ── Shared RAG build context ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Both rag-server and indexer build from the rag-service repo.
# RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH must point to your local clone of the rag-service repo.
x-rag-build: &rag-build
  context: ${RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH:?Set RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH to the local path of your rag-service clone (required for --profile rag)}
  dockerfile_inline: |
    FROM python:3.12-slim
    WORKDIR /app
    RUN pip install --no-cache-dir uv
    COPY requirements.txt .
    RUN uv pip install --system --no-cache -r requirements.txt
    COPY . .
    RUN uv pip install --system --no-cache -e .

# ── Services ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
services:
  agent-1:
    <<: *agent-base
    environment:
      AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
      WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
      WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
      GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
      SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
      SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
      GIT_AUTHOR_NAME: "Agent 1"
      GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL: "${GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL}"
      MCP_RAG_URL: "${MCP_RAG_URL:-}"

  # agent-2:
  #   <<: *agent-base
  #   environment:
  #     AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
  #     WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
  #     WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
  #     GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
  #     SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
  #     SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
  #     GIT_AUTHOR_NAME: "Agent 2"
  #     GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL: "${GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL}"
  #     MCP_RAG_URL: "${MCP_RAG_URL:-}"

  # ── RAG stack (optional — start with: docker compose --profile rag up) ────────
  qdrant:
    image: qdrant/qdrant:latest
    profiles: [rag]
    ports:
      - "6333:6333"
      - "6334:6334"
    volumes:
      - qdrant_data:/qdrant/storage
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: "2"
          memory: 2G
        reservations:
          cpus: "0.25"
          memory: 256M

  rag-server:
    build: *rag-build
    profiles: [rag]
    command: ["python", "-m", "services.rag_server.main"]
    depends_on:
      - qdrant
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    environment:
      QDRANT_URL: "${QDRANT_URL:-http://qdrant:6333}"

        limits:
          cpus: "2"
          memory: 4G
        reservations:
          cpus: "0.5"
          memory: 1G

  indexer:
    build: *rag-build
    profiles: [rag]
    command: ["python", "-m", "services.indexer.main"]
    depends_on:
      - qdrant
    environment:
      QDRANT_URL: "${QDRANT_URL:-http://qdrant:6333}"
      WORKSPACE_ID: "${WORKSPACE_ID:?WORKSPACE_ID must be set — e.g. 'workspace'}"
      INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS: "${INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS:-300}"
      # Comma-separated paths of repos to index inside the container.
      # Mount the repos you want indexed (e.g. management repo + workflow repo).
      # Example: REPO_PATHS=/repos/workspace,/repos/workflow
      REPO_PATHS: "${REPO_PATHS:?REPO_PATHS must be set — comma-separated list of repo paths to index}"
    volumes:
      - workspaces:/repos/workspaces:ro
      - workflow:/repos/workflow:ro
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: "2"
          memory: 4G
        reservations:
          cpus: "0.5"
          memory: 1G

# ── Volumes ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
volumes:
  workspaces:
    driver: local
  workflow:
    driver: local
  qdrant_data:
    driver: local

---
name: init-agent
description: Set up a new local agent compute environment — configures Docker Compose, .env, agent.yaml, and optionally provisions the RAG stack (qdrant, rag-server, indexer).
---

## Purpose

Guide an operator through the one-time setup of a local agent host using Docker Compose.
This skill is the canonical setup path for new agent deployments.

Scope: agent compute (Docker Compose stack). For workspace setup (management repo, feature
tracking), use `init-workspace` instead.

---

## Prerequisites

Verify these before starting. Stop if any are missing and tell the operator what is needed.

| Prerequisite | How to check |
|---|---|
| Docker 24+ with Buildx | `docker buildx version` |
| Docker Compose v2 | `docker compose version` |
| Anthropic API key | `console.anthropic.com` |
| GitHub PAT with `repo` scope | `github.com/settings/tokens` |
| SSH key with read/write access to workspace repos | `ssh -T git@github.com` |

---

## Step 1 — Locate the compose directory

```bash
# From the workflow/ repo root:
cd agent-runtime/orchestration/local
```

All subsequent steps run from this directory.

---

## Step 2 — Configure `.env`

```bash
cp .env.example .env
```

Collect and write the following values. Ask one group at a time.

### Required

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | Anthropic API key (`sk-ant-...`) |
| `GITHUB_TOKEN` | GitHub PAT with `repo` scope (`ghp_...`) |
| `GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL` | Email for git identity (task log attribution) |
| `GIT_AUTHOR_NAME` | Name for git identity |
| `WORKFLOW_URL` | SSH URL of the workflow repo (cloned at startup) |

### SSH key delivery (choose one)

**Option A — env var (preferred for CI/K8s):**
```env
SSH_PRIVATE_KEY=-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
...key content...
-----END OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
```

**Option B — file mount (Docker Compose local use):**
```env
SSH_KEY_DIR=~/.ssh
```

---

## Step 3 — Configure `agent.yaml`

```bash
cp agent.yaml.example agent.yaml
```

Collect and write:

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `watches` | SSH URL(s) of the workspace management repos this agent should pick tasks from |
| `jitter_max_seconds` | Pre-claim jitter; increase when running many agents (e.g. `15`) |
| `budget.max_tokens_per_task` | Token budget per task (default: `100000`) |
| `budget.max_iterations` | Iteration cap per task (default: `50`) |
| `idle_sleep_seconds` | Poll interval in seconds (default: `60`; set `0` for single-shot mode) |

---

## Step 4 — RAG stack (optional)

Ask the operator: _"Do you want to enable the RAG context stack? It adds three services
(qdrant, rag-server, indexer) that give agents searchable project knowledge. Agents
work without it — they just start with cold context."_

If **yes**, continue to Step 4a. If **no**, skip to Step 5.

### Step 4a — RAG prerequisites

| Prerequisite | Description |
|---|---|
| `rag-service` repo cloned locally | The rag-server and indexer images are built from this repo |

Ask:
- **RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH** — local filesystem path to the `rag-service` repo clone

### Step 4b — RAG environment variables

Add to `.env`:

```env
# RAG stack
RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH=/path/to/your/rag-service

# Workspace ID — must match workspace_id in your workspace.yaml (e.g. workspace)
WORKSPACE_ID=workspace

# Comma-separated paths of repos to index (container-internal paths).
# The indexer mounts workspaces at /repos/workspaces and workflow at /repos/workflow.
# Example: your management repo clone is at /repos/workspaces/<workspace-id>
REPO_PATHS=/repos/workspaces/<workspace-id>,/repos/workflow

# Optional overrides (defaults shown):
# QDRANT_URL=http://qdrant:6333
# MCP_RAG_URL=http://rag-server:8000
# INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS=300
```

Explain to the operator:
- `WORKSPACE_ID` isolates this workspace's index in Qdrant — multiple workspaces can share one Qdrant instance
- `REPO_PATHS` tells the indexer which repos to watch; the indexer mounts the compose `workspaces` volume at `/repos/workspaces`
- `MCP_RAG_URL` is passed to agents so they can call `rag_query` mid-task; the default `http://rag-server:8000` works when the full stack runs in the same compose project

---

## Step 5 — Start the stack

### Without RAG (agents only):

```bash
docker compose up --build
```

### With RAG stack:

```bash
docker compose --profile rag up --build
```

**First-start note:** The `rag-server` and `indexer` images download
`sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2` (~90 MB) on their first build. Subsequent
starts are fast. The index is empty until the indexer completes its first polling cycle
(up to `INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS` = 5 min by default). Agents degrade gracefully
during this warm-up period.

---

## Step 6 — Verify

Run these checks after the stack starts:

```bash
# All containers running?
docker compose ps

# Agent picking up work?
docker compose logs agent-1 | tail -20

# RAG server reachable (if --profile rag)?
curl -s http://localhost:8000/health | jq .

# Qdrant reachable (if --profile rag)?
curl -s http://localhost:6333/healthz
```

Expected output:
- `agent-1` shows `bootstrap_started` then `poll_workspace_pulled` in JSON logs
- `rag-server` health endpoint returns `{"status": "ok"}`
- `qdrant` healthz returns `healthz check passed`

---

## Step 7 — Add a second agent (optional)

Uncomment the `agent-2` block in `docker-compose.yml`, then re-run:

```bash
docker compose --profile rag up --build   # or without --profile rag
```

Both agents share the same `workspaces` volume and use the same Qdrant collection —
they do not diverge.

---

## Common issues

| Symptom | Likely cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH must be set` error | RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH missing from .env | Add it and re-run with `--profile rag` |
| `WORKSPACE_ID must be set` error | WORKSPACE_ID missing from .env | Add it (must match workspace.yaml workspace_id) |
| `rag-server` fails on startup | Qdrant not yet ready | Wait 10–15 s; rag-server retries automatically |
| Agents log `MCP_RAG_URL` connection refused | RAG stack not started | Start with `--profile rag` or unset `MCP_RAG_URL` for cold start |
| `bootstrap_failed: git_workspace_sync_failed` | SSH key can't reach workspace repo | Verify SSH key in .env; check `ssh -T git@github.com` |
| `skill_reference_audit` warnings | A skill slug in tasks.md has no matching directory | Non-fatal — agent skips that task |

---

## Stopping and resetting

```bash
# Stop all containers (keep volumes):
docker compose down

# Stop and wipe all volumes (forces full re-clone and re-index on next start):
docker compose down -v
```

---

## Production deployment

The local Docker Compose is for development only. For production, see:

| Target | Path |
|---|---|
| Kubernetes | `orchestration/kubernetes/deployment.yaml` |
| GitHub Actions | `orchestration/github-actions/scheduled.yml` |

In production:
- Replace `QDRANT_URL=http://qdrant:6333` with your Qdrant VM address (`http://<vm-ip>:6333`)
- Replace `MCP_RAG_URL=http://rag-server:8000` with your rag-server pod address
- The rag-server and indexer images should be pre-built and pushed to a registry
- No code change is required between local and production — only `QDRANT_URL` and `MCP_RAG_URL` change

# Agent-runtime — local multi-agent compose
#
# Two long-running agent services. Each runs a continuous internal polling loop,
# pulling workspace state and scanning for ready tasks on every cycle.
# Agent-2 is commented out by default; uncomment to run two agents in parallel.

name: agent-runtime-local

# ── Shared agent definition ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
x-agent: &agent-base
  build:
    # Build context is workflow/ root so schemas/ is included.
    # Path is three levels up: local/ → orchestration/ → agent-runtime/ → workflow/
    context: ../../..
    dockerfile: agent-runtime/Dockerfile
  env_file: .env
  environment:
    AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
    WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
    WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
    # Required for the pr-create skill (GitHub REST API).
    GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
    # SSH key delivery: SSH_PRIVATE_KEY (env var, preferred) or SSH_KEY_PATH (file mount).
    SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
    SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
    # RAG context injection — optional. When set, the agent queries the RAG server
    # for relevant project context before starting each task.
    # Empty (default) = graceful cold start — no rag_query calls attempted.
    # Set in .env: MCP_RAG_URL=http://rag-server:8000 (when --profile rag is used).
    MCP_RAG_URL: "${MCP_RAG_URL:-}"
  volumes:
    - ./agent.yaml:/agent/agent.yaml:ro
    - workspaces:/agent/data
    - workflow:/agent/workflow
    - ${SSH_KEY_DIR:-${HOME}/.ssh}:/agent/ssh:ro
  restart: unless-stopped
  deploy:
    resources:
      limits:
        cpus: "4"
        memory: 10G
      reservations:
        cpus: "1"
        memory: 2G

# ── Shared RAG build context ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Both rag-server and indexer build from the rag-service repo.
# RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH must point to your local clone of the rag-service repo.
x-rag-build: &rag-build
  context: ${RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH:?Set RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH to the local path of your rag-service clone (required for --profile rag)}
  dockerfile_inline: |
    FROM python:3.12-slim
    WORKDIR /app
    RUN pip install --no-cache-dir uv
    COPY requirements.txt .
    RUN uv pip install --system --no-cache -r requirements.txt
    COPY . .
    RUN uv pip install --system --no-cache -e .

# ── Services ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
services:
  agent-1:
    <<: *agent-base
    environment:
      AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
      WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
      WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
      GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
      SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
      SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
      GIT_AUTHOR_NAME: "Agent 1"
      GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL: "${GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL}"
      MCP_RAG_URL: "${MCP_RAG_URL:-}"

  # agent-2:
  #   <<: *agent-base
  #   environment:
  #     AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
  #     WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
  #     WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
  #     GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
  #     SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
  #     SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
  #     GIT_AUTHOR_NAME: "Agent 2"
  #     GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL: "${GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL}"
  #     MCP_RAG_URL: "${MCP_RAG_URL:-}"

  # ── RAG stack (optional — start with: docker compose --profile rag up) ────────
  qdrant:
    image: qdrant/qdrant:latest
    profiles: [rag]
    ports:
      - "6333:6333"
      - "6334:6334"
    volumes:
      - qdrant_data:/qdrant/storage
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: "2"
          memory: 2G
        reservations:
          cpus: "0.25"
          memory: 256M

  rag-server:
    build: *rag-build
    profiles: [rag]
    command: ["python", "-m", "services.rag_server.main"]
    depends_on:
      - qdrant
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    environment:
      QDRANT_URL: "${QDRANT_URL:-http://qdrant:6333}"
      WORKSPACE_ID: "${WORKSPACE_ID:?WORKSPACE_ID must be set — e.g. 'workspace'}"
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: "2"
          memory: 4G
        reservations:
          cpus: "0.5"
          memory: 1G

  indexer:
    build: *rag-build
    profiles: [rag]
    command: ["python", "-m", "services.indexer.main"]
    depends_on:
      - qdrant
    environment:
      QDRANT_URL: "${QDRANT_URL:-http://qdrant:6333}"
      WORKSPACE_ID: "${WORKSPACE_ID:?WORKSPACE_ID must be set — e.g. 'workspace'}"
      INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS: "${INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS:-300}"
      # Comma-separated paths of repos to index inside the container.
      # Mount the repos you want indexed (e.g. management repo + workflow repo).
      # Example: REPO_PATHS=/repos/workspace,/repos/workflow
      REPO_PATHS: "${REPO_PATHS:?REPO_PATHS must be set — comma-separated list of repo paths to index}"
    volumes:
      - workspaces:/repos/workspaces:ro
      - workflow:/repos/workflow:ro
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: "2"
          memory: 4G
        reservations:
          cpus: "0.5"
          memory: 1G

# ── Volumes ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
volumes:
  workspaces:
    driver: local
  workflow:
    driver: local
  qdrant_data:
    driver: local

# Agent-runtime — local multi-agent compose
#
# Two long-running agent services plus RAG stack (qdrant, rag-server, indexer).
# The RAG stack is included by default — all services start with `docker compose up --build`.
# Agent-2 is commented out by default; uncomment to run two agents in parallel.
#
# restart: unless-stopped provides crash recovery only — the poll cadence is
# controlled by idle_sleep_seconds in agent.yaml (default: 60 s), not by
# container restarts.
#
# RAG stack (qdrant + rag-server + indexer) is enabled by default. Required:
#   1. Set RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH in .env to the path of your local rag-service clone.
#   2. Set WORKSPACE_ID in .env (e.g. "workspace").
#   3. Set REPO_PATHS in .env to comma-separated paths of repos to index
#      (e.g. "/repos/workspaces/my-workspace,/repos/workflow").
#
# Usage (from this directory):
#   cp agent.yaml.example agent.yaml   # fill in watches: with your workspace SSH URL
#   cp .env.example .env               # fill in required values (including RAG vars)
#   docker compose up --build          # start agents + RAG stack (default)
#   docker compose up --build -d       # start in background

name: agent-runtime-local

# ── Shared agent definition ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
x-agent: &agent-base
  build:
    # Build context is workflow/ root so schemas/ is included.
    # Path is three levels up: local/ → orchestration/ → agent-runtime/ → workflow/
    context: ../../..
    dockerfile: agent-runtime/Dockerfile
  env_file: .env
  environment:
    AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
    WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
    WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
    # Required for the pr-create skill (GitHub REST API).
    GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
    # SSH key delivery: SSH_PRIVATE_KEY (env var, preferred) or SSH_KEY_PATH (file mount).
    SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
    SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
    # RAG context injection — enabled by default. Agents query the RAG server
    # for relevant project context before starting each task.
    # Defaults to the companion rag-server in this compose file.
    # Override in .env to point to an external RAG server, or set to empty to disable.
    MCP_RAG_URL: "${MCP_RAG_URL:-http://rag-server:8000}"
  volumes:
    - ./agent.yaml:/agent/agent.yaml:ro
    - workspaces:/agent/data
    - workflow:/agent/workflow
    - ${SSH_KEY_DIR:-${HOME}/.ssh}:/agent/ssh:ro
  restart: unless-stopped
  deploy:
    resources:
      limits:
        cpus: "4"
        memory: 10G
      reservations:
        cpus: "1"
        memory: 2G

# ── Shared RAG build context ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Both rag-server and indexer build from the rag-service repo.
# RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH must point to your local clone of the rag-service repo.

  dockerfile_inline: |

  qdrant:

---
name: init-agent
description: Set up a new local agent compute environment — configures Docker Compose, .env, agent.yaml, and optionally provisions the RAG stack (qdrant, rag-server, indexer).
---

## Purpose

Guide an operator through the one-time setup of a local agent host using Docker Compose.
This skill is the canonical setup path for new agent deployments.

Scope: agent compute (Docker Compose stack). For workspace setup (management repo, feature
tracking), use `init-workspace` instead.

---

## Prerequisites

Verify these before starting. Stop if any are missing and tell the operator what is needed.

| Prerequisite | How to check |
|---|---|
| Docker 24+ with Buildx | `docker buildx version` |
| Docker Compose v2 | `docker compose version` |
| Anthropic API key | `console.anthropic.com` |
| GitHub PAT with `repo` scope | `github.com/settings/tokens` |
| SSH key with read/write access to workspace repos | `ssh -T git@github.com` |

---

## Step 1 — Locate the compose directory

```bash
# From the workflow/ repo root:
cd agent-runtime/orchestration/local
```

All subsequent steps run from this directory.

---

## Step 2 — Configure `.env`

```bash
cp .env.example .env
```

Collect and write the following values. Ask one group at a time.

### Required

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | Anthropic API key (`sk-ant-...`) |
| `GITHUB_TOKEN` | GitHub PAT with `repo` scope (`ghp_...`) |
| `GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL` | Email for git identity (task log attribution) |
| `GIT_AUTHOR_NAME` | Name for git identity |
| `WORKFLOW_URL` | SSH URL of the workflow repo (cloned at startup) |

### SSH key delivery (choose one)

**Option A — env var (preferred for CI/K8s):**
```env
SSH_PRIVATE_KEY=-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
...key content...
-----END OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
```

**Option B — file mount (Docker Compose local use):**
```env
SSH_KEY_DIR=~/.ssh
```

---

## Step 3 — Configure `agent.yaml`

```bash
cp agent.yaml.example agent.yaml
```

Collect and write:

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `watches` | SSH URL(s) of the workspace management repos this agent should pick tasks from |
| `jitter_max_seconds` | Pre-claim jitter; increase when running many agents (e.g. `15`) |
| `budget.max_tokens_per_task` | Token budget per task (default: `100000`) |
| `budget.max_iterations` | Iteration cap per task (default: `50`) |
| `idle_sleep_seconds` | Poll interval in seconds (default: `60`; set `0` for single-shot mode) |

---

## Step 4 — RAG stack (optional)

Ask the operator: _"Do you want to enable the RAG context stack? It adds three services
(qdrant, rag-server, indexer) that give agents searchable project knowledge. Agents
work without it — they just start with cold context."_

If **yes**, continue to Step 4a. If **no**, skip to Step 5.

### Step 4a — RAG prerequisites

| Prerequisite | Description |
|---|---|
| `rag-service` repo cloned locally | The rag-server and indexer images are built from this repo |

Ask:
- **RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH** — local filesystem path to the `rag-service` repo clone

### Step 4b — RAG environment variables

Add to `.env`:

```env
# RAG stack
RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH=/path/to/your/rag-service

# Workspace ID — must match workspace_id in your workspace.yaml (e.g. workspace)
WORKSPACE_ID=workspace

# Comma-separated paths of repos to index (container-internal paths).
# The indexer mounts workspaces at /repos/workspaces and workflow at /repos/workflow.
# Example: your management repo clone is at /repos/workspaces/<workspace-id>
REPO_PATHS=/repos/workspaces/<workspace-id>,/repos/workflow

# Optional overrides (defaults shown):
# QDRANT_URL=http://qdrant:6333
# MCP_RAG_URL=http://rag-server:8000
# INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS=300
```

Explain to the operator:
- `WORKSPACE_ID` isolates this workspace's index in Qdrant — multiple workspaces can share one Qdrant instance
- `REPO_PATHS` tells the indexer which repos to watch; the indexer mounts the compose `workspaces` volume at `/repos/workspaces`
- `MCP_RAG_URL` is passed to agents so they can call `rag_query` mid-task; the default `http://rag-server:8000` works when the full stack runs in the same compose project

---

## Step 5 — Start the stack

### Without RAG (agents only):

```bash
docker compose up --build
```

### With RAG stack:

```bash
docker compose --profile rag up --build
```

**First-start note:** The `rag-server` and `indexer` images download
`sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2` (~90 MB) on their first build. Subsequent
starts are fast. The index is empty until the indexer completes its first polling cycle
(up to `INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS` = 5 min by default). Agents degrade gracefully
during this warm-up period.

---

## Step 6 — Verify

Run these checks after the stack starts:

```bash

docker compose ps

# Agent picking up work?
docker compose logs agent-1 | tail -20

# RAG server reachable (if --profile rag)?
curl -s http://localhost:8000/health | jq .

# Qdrant reachable (if --profile rag)?
curl -s http://localhost:6333/healthz
```

Expected output:
- `agent-1` shows `bootstrap_started` then `poll_workspace_pulled` in JSON logs
- `rag-server` health endpoint returns `{"status": "ok"}`
- `qdrant` healthz returns `healthz check passed`

---

## Step 7 — Add a second agent (optional)

Uncomment the `agent-2` block in `docker-compose.yml`, then re-run:

```bash

```

Both agents share the same `workspaces` volume and use the same Qdrant collection —
they do not diverge.

---

## Common issues

| Symptom | Likely cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH must be set` error | RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH missing from .env | Add it and re-run with `--profile rag` |
| `WORKSPACE_ID must be set` error | WORKSPACE_ID missing from .env | Add it (must match workspace.yaml workspace_id) |
| `rag-server` fails on startup | Qdrant not yet ready | Wait 10–15 s; rag-server retries automatically |
| Agents log `MCP_RAG_URL` connection refused | RAG stack not started | Start with `--profile rag` or unset `MCP_RAG_URL` for cold start |
| `bootstrap_failed: git_workspace_sync_failed` | SSH key can't reach workspace repo | Verify SSH key in .env; check `ssh -T git@github.com` |
| `skill_reference_audit` warnings | A skill slug in tasks.md has no matching directory | Non-fatal — agent skips that task |

---

## Stopping and resetting

```bash
# Stop all containers (keep volumes):
docker compose down

# Stop and wipe all volumes (forces full re-clone and re-index on next start):
docker compose down -v
```

---

## Production deployment

The local Docker Compose is for development only. For production, see:

| Target | Path |
|---|---|
| Kubernetes | `orchestration/kubernetes/deployment.yaml` |
| GitHub Actions | `orchestration/github-actions/scheduled.yml` |

In production:
- Replace `QDRANT_URL=http://qdrant:6333` with your Qdrant VM address (`http://<vm-ip>:6333`)
- Replace `MCP_RAG_URL=http://rag-server:8000` with your rag-server pod address
- The rag-server and indexer images should be pre-built and pushed to a registry
- No code change is required between local and production — only `QDRANT_URL` and `MCP_RAG_URL` change

name: init-agent
description: Set up a new local agent compute environment — configures Docker Compose, .env, agent.yaml, and the RAG stack (qdrant, rag-server, indexer) which is enabled by default.
---

## Purpose

Guide an operator through the one-time setup of a local agent host using Docker Compose.
This skill is the canonical setup path for new agent deployments.

Scope: agent compute (Docker Compose stack). For workspace setup (management repo, feature
tracking), use `init-workspace` instead.

---

## Prerequisites

Verify these before starting. Stop if any are missing and tell the operator what is needed.

| Prerequisite | How to check |
|---|---|
| Docker 24+ with Buildx | `docker buildx version` |
| Docker Compose v2 | `docker compose version` |
| Anthropic API key | `console.anthropic.com` |
| GitHub PAT with `repo` scope | `github.com/settings/tokens` |
| SSH key with read/write access to workspace repos | `ssh -T git@github.com` |

---

## Step 1 — Locate the compose directory

```bash
# From the workflow/ repo root:
cd agent-runtime/orchestration/local
```

All subsequent steps run from this directory.

---

## Step 2 — Configure `.env`

```bash
cp .env.example .env
```

Collect and write the following values. Ask one group at a time.

### Required

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | Anthropic API key (`sk-ant-...`) |
| `GITHUB_TOKEN` | GitHub PAT with `repo` scope (`ghp_...`) |
| `GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL` | Email for git identity (task log attribution) |
| `GIT_AUTHOR_NAME` | Name for git identity |
| `WORKFLOW_URL` | SSH URL of the workflow repo (cloned at startup) |

### SSH key delivery (choose one)

**Option A — env var (preferred for CI/K8s):**
```env
SSH_PRIVATE_KEY=-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
...key content...
-----END OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
```

**Option B — file mount (Docker Compose local use):**
```env
SSH_KEY_DIR=~/.ssh
```

---

## Step 3 — Configure `agent.yaml`

```bash
cp agent.yaml.example agent.yaml
```

Collect and write:

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `watches` | SSH URL(s) of the workspace management repos this agent should pick tasks from |
| `jitter_max_seconds` | Pre-claim jitter; increase when running many agents (e.g. `15`) |
| `budget.max_tokens_per_task` | Token budget per task (default: `100000`) |
| `budget.max_iterations` | Iteration cap per task (default: `50`) |
| `idle_sleep_seconds` | Poll interval in seconds (default: `60`; set `0` for single-shot mode) |

---

## Step 4 — RAG stack (optional)

Ask the operator: _"Do you want to enable the RAG context stack? It adds three services
(qdrant, rag-server, indexer) that give agents searchable project knowledge. Agents
work without it — they just start with cold context."_

If **yes**, continue to Step 4a. If **no**, skip to Step 5.

### Step 4a — RAG prerequisites

| Prerequisite | Description |
|---|---|
| `rag-service` repo cloned locally | The rag-server and indexer images are built from this repo |

Ask:
- **RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH** — local filesystem path to the `rag-service` repo clone

### Step 4b — RAG environment variables

Add to `.env`:

```env
# RAG stack
RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH=/path/to/your/rag-service

# Workspace ID — must match workspace_id in your workspace.yaml (e.g. workspace)
WORKSPACE_ID=workspace

# Comma-separated paths of repos to index (container-internal paths).
# The indexer mounts workspaces at /repos/workspaces and workflow at /repos/workflow.
# Example: your management repo clone is at /repos/workspaces/<workspace-id>
REPO_PATHS=/repos/workspaces/<workspace-id>,/repos/workflow

# Optional overrides (defaults shown):
# QDRANT_URL=http://qdrant:6333
# MCP_RAG_URL=http://rag-server:8000
# INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS=300
```

Explain to the operator:
- `WORKSPACE_ID` isolates this workspace's index in Qdrant — multiple workspaces can share one Qdrant instance
- `REPO_PATHS` tells the indexer which repos to watch; the indexer mounts the compose `workspaces` volume at `/repos/workspaces`
- `MCP_RAG_URL` is passed to agents so they can call `rag_query` mid-task; the default `http://rag-server:8000` works when the full stack runs in the same compose project

---

## Step 5 — Start the stack

### Without RAG (agents only):

```bash
docker compose up --build
```

### With RAG stack:

```bash
docker compose --profile rag up --build
```

**First-start note:** The `rag-server` and `indexer` images download
`sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2` (~90 MB) on their first build. Subsequent
starts are fast. The index is empty until the indexer completes its first polling cycle
(up to `INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS` = 5 min by default). Agents degrade gracefully
during this warm-up period.

---

## Step 6 — Verify

Run these checks after the stack starts:

```bash
# All containers running?
docker compose ps

# Agent picking up work?
docker compose logs agent-1 | tail -20

# RAG server reachable (if --profile rag)?
curl -s http://localhost:8000/health | jq .

# Qdrant reachable (if --profile rag)?
curl -s http://localhost:6333/healthz
```

Expected output:
- `agent-1` shows `bootstrap_started` then `poll_workspace_pulled` in JSON logs
- `rag-server` health endpoint returns `{"status": "ok"}`
- `qdrant` healthz returns `healthz check passed`

---

## Step 7 — Add a second agent (optional)

Uncomment the `agent-2` block in `docker-compose.yml`, then re-run:

```bash
docker compose --profile rag up --build   # or without --profile rag
```

Both agents share the same `workspaces` volume and use the same Qdrant collection —
they do not diverge.

---

## Common issues

| Symptom | Likely cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH must be set` error | RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH missing from .env | Add it and re-run with `--profile rag` |
| `WORKSPACE_ID must be set` error | WORKSPACE_ID missing from .env | Add it (must match workspace.yaml workspace_id) |

| `bootstrap_failed: git_workspace_sync_failed` | SSH key can't reach workspace repo | Verify SSH key in .env; check `ssh -T git@github.com` |
| `skill_reference_audit` warnings | A skill slug in tasks.md has no matching directory | Non-fatal — agent skips that task |

---

## Stopping and resetting

```bash
# Stop all containers (keep volumes):
docker compose down

# Stop and wipe all volumes (forces full re-clone and re-index on next start):
docker compose down -v
```

---

## Production deployment

The local Docker Compose is for development only. For production, see:

| Target | Path |
|---|---|
| Kubernetes | `orchestration/kubernetes/deployment.yaml` |
| GitHub Actions | `orchestration/github-actions/scheduled.yml` |

In production:
- Replace `QDRANT_URL=http://qdrant:6333` with your Qdrant VM address (`http://<vm-ip>:6333`)
- Replace `MCP_RAG_URL=http://rag-server:8000` with your rag-server pod address
- The rag-server and indexer images should be pre-built and pushed to a registry
- No code change is required between local and production — only `QDRANT_URL` and `MCP_RAG_URL` change

description: Set up a new local agent compute environment — configures Docker Compose, .env, agent.yaml, and the RAG stack (qdrant, rag-server, indexer) which is enabled by default.
---

## Purpose

Guide an operator through the one-time setup of a local agent host using Docker Compose.
This skill is the canonical setup path for new agent deployments.

Scope: agent compute (Docker Compose stack). For workspace setup (management repo, feature
tracking), use `init-workspace` instead.

---

## Prerequisites

Verify these before starting. Stop if any are missing and tell the operator what is needed.

| Prerequisite | How to check |
|---|---|
| Docker 24+ with Buildx | `docker buildx version` |
| Docker Compose v2 | `docker compose version` |
| Anthropic API key | `console.anthropic.com` |
| GitHub PAT with `repo` scope | `github.com/settings/tokens` |
| SSH key with read/write access to workspace repos | `ssh -T git@github.com` |

---

## Step 1 — Locate the compose directory

```bash
# From the workflow/ repo root:
cd agent-runtime/orchestration/local
```

All subsequent steps run from this directory.

---

## Step 2 — Configure `.env`

```bash
cp .env.example .env
```

Collect and write the following values. Ask one group at a time.

### Required

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | Anthropic API key (`sk-ant-...`) |
| `GITHUB_TOKEN` | GitHub PAT with `repo` scope (`ghp_...`) |
| `GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL` | Email for git identity (task log attribution) |
| `GIT_AUTHOR_NAME` | Name for git identity |
| `WORKFLOW_URL` | SSH URL of the workflow repo (cloned at startup) |

### SSH key delivery (choose one)

**Option A — env var (preferred for CI/K8s):**
```env
SSH_PRIVATE_KEY=-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
...key content...
-----END OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
```

**Option B — file mount (Docker Compose local use):**
```env
SSH_KEY_DIR=~/.ssh
```

---

## Step 3 — Configure `agent.yaml`

```bash
cp agent.yaml.example agent.yaml
```

Collect and write:

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `watches` | SSH URL(s) of the workspace management repos this agent should pick tasks from |
| `jitter_max_seconds` | Pre-claim jitter; increase when running many agents (e.g. `15`) |
| `budget.max_tokens_per_task` | Token budget per task (default: `100000`) |
| `budget.max_iterations` | Iteration cap per task (default: `50`) |
| `idle_sleep_seconds` | Poll interval in seconds (default: `60`; set `0` for single-shot mode) |

---

## Step 4 — RAG stack (optional, enabled by default)

The RAG stack (qdrant, rag-server, indexer) is included in the default compose setup
and gives agents searchable project knowledge at claim time. All three services start
with the standard `docker compose up --build` — no extra flags needed.

Inform the operator: _"The RAG context stack is enabled by default. It requires a local
clone of the `rag-service` repo. If you don't have one or want to skip RAG for now,
you can comment out the qdrant, rag-server, and indexer services in docker-compose.yml."_

Continue to Step 4a to configure the RAG variables. If the operator chooses to skip
RAG entirely, jump to Step 5 and leave the RAG variables unset.

### Step 4a — RAG prerequisites

| Prerequisite | Description |
|---|---|
| `rag-service` repo cloned locally | The rag-server and indexer images are built from this repo |

Ask:
- **RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH** — local filesystem path to the `rag-service` repo clone

### Step 4b — RAG environment variables

Add to `.env`:

```env
# RAG stack
RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH=/path/to/your/rag-service

# Workspace ID — must match workspace_id in your workspace.yaml (e.g. workspace)
WORKSPACE_ID=workspace

# Comma-separated paths of repos to index (container-internal paths).
# The indexer mounts workspaces at /repos/workspaces and workflow at /repos/workflow.
# Example: your management repo clone is at /repos/workspaces/<workspace-id>
REPO_PATHS=/repos/workspaces/<workspace-id>,/repos/workflow

# Optional overrides (defaults shown):
# QDRANT_URL=http://qdrant:6333
# MCP_RAG_URL=http://rag-server:8000
# INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS=300
```

Explain to the operator:
- `WORKSPACE_ID` isolates this workspace's index in Qdrant — multiple workspaces can share one Qdrant instance
- `REPO_PATHS` tells the indexer which repos to watch; the indexer mounts the compose `workspaces` volume at `/repos/workspaces`
- `MCP_RAG_URL` is passed to agents so they can call `rag_query` mid-task; the default `http://rag-server:8000` works when the full stack runs in the same compose project

---

## Step 5 — Start the stack

```bash
docker compose up --build
```

This starts agents plus the full RAG stack (qdrant, rag-server, indexer) by default.

**First-start note:** The `rag-server` and `indexer` images download
`sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2` (~90 MB) on their first build. Subsequent
starts are fast. The index is empty until the indexer completes its first polling cycle
(up to `INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS` = 5 min by default). Agents degrade gracefully
during this warm-up period.

---

## Step 6 — Verify

Run these checks after the stack starts:

```bash
# All containers running?
docker compose ps

# Agent picking up work?
docker compose logs agent-1 | tail -20

# RAG server reachable (if --profile rag)?
curl -s http://localhost:8000/health | jq .

# Qdrant reachable (if --profile rag)?
curl -s http://localhost:6333/healthz
```

Expected output:
- `agent-1` shows `bootstrap_started` then `poll_workspace_pulled` in JSON logs
- `rag-server` health endpoint returns `{"status": "ok"}`
- `qdrant` healthz returns `healthz check passed`

---

## Step 7 — Add a second agent (optional)

Uncomment the `agent-2` block in `docker-compose.yml`, then re-run:

```bash
docker compose --profile rag up --build   # or without --profile rag
```

Both agents share the same `workspaces` volume and use the same Qdrant collection —
they do not diverge.

---

## Common issues

| Symptom | Likely cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH must be set` error | RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH missing from .env | Add it and re-run with `--profile rag` |
| `WORKSPACE_ID must be set` error | WORKSPACE_ID missing from .env | Add it (must match workspace.yaml workspace_id) |
| `rag-server` fails on startup | Qdrant not yet ready | Wait 10–15 s; rag-server retries automatically |
| Agents log `MCP_RAG_URL` connection refused | RAG stack not started | Start with `--profile rag` or unset `MCP_RAG_URL` for cold start |
| `bootstrap_failed: git_workspace_sync_failed` | SSH key can't reach workspace repo | Verify SSH key in .env; check `ssh -T git@github.com` |
| `skill_reference_audit` warnings | A skill slug in tasks.md has no matching directory | Non-fatal — agent skips that task |

---

## Stopping and resetting

```bash
# Stop all containers (keep volumes):
docker compose down

# Stop and wipe all volumes (forces full re-clone and re-index on next start):
docker compose down -v
```

---

## Production deployment

The local Docker Compose is for development only. For production, see:

| Target | Path |
|---|---|
| Kubernetes | `orchestration/kubernetes/deployment.yaml` |
| GitHub Actions | `orchestration/github-actions/scheduled.yml` |

In production:
- Replace `QDRANT_URL=http://qdrant:6333` with your Qdrant VM address (`http://<vm-ip>:6333`)
- Replace `MCP_RAG_URL=http://rag-server:8000` with your rag-server pod address
- The rag-server and indexer images should be pre-built and pushed to a registry
- No code change is required between local and production — only `QDRANT_URL` and `MCP_RAG_URL` change

file.content:
# Agent-runtime — local multi-agent compose
#
# Two long-running agent services plus RAG stack (qdrant, rag-server, indexer).
# The RAG stack is included by default — all services start with `docker compose up --build`.
# Agent-2 is commented out by default; uncomment to run two agents in parallel.
#
# restart: unless-stopped provides crash recovery only — the poll cadence is
# controlled by idle_sleep_seconds in agent.yaml (default: 60 s), not by
# container restarts.
#
# RAG stack (qdrant + rag-server + indexer) is enabled by default. Required:
#   1. Set RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH in .env to the path of your local rag-service clone.
#   2. Set WORKSPACE_ID in .env (e.g. "workspace").
#   3. Set REPO_PATHS in .env to comma-separated paths of repos to index
#      (e.g. "/repos/workspaces/my-workspace,/repos/workflow").
#
# Usage (from this directory):
#   cp agent.yaml.example agent.yaml   # fill in watches: with your workspace SSH URL
#   cp .env.example .env               # fill in required values (including RAG vars)
#   docker compose up --build          # start agents + RAG stack (default)
#   docker compose up --build -d       # start in background

name: agent-runtime-local

# ── Shared agent definition ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
x-agent: &agent-base
  build:
    # Build context is workflow/ root so schemas/ is included.
    # Path is three levels up: local/ → orchestration/ → agent-runtime/ → workflow/
    context: ../../..
    dockerfile: agent-runtime/Dockerfile
  env_file: .env
  environment:
    AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
    WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
    WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
    # Required for the pr-create skill (GitHub REST API).
    GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
    # SSH key delivery: SSH_PRIVATE_KEY (env var, preferred) or SSH_KEY_PATH (file mount).
    SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
    SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
    # RAG context injection — enabled by default. Agents query the RAG server
    # for relevant project context before starting each task.
    # Defaults to the companion rag-server in this compose file.
    # Override in .env to point to an external RAG server, or set to empty to disable.
    MCP_RAG_URL: "${MCP_RAG_URL:-http://rag-server:8000}"
  volumes:
    - ./agent.yaml:/agent/agent.yaml:ro
    - workspaces:/agent/data
    - workflow:/agent/workflow
    - ${SSH_KEY_DIR:-${HOME}/.ssh}:/agent/ssh:ro
  restart: unless-stopped
  deploy:
    resources:
      limits:
        cpus: "4"
        memory: 10G
      reservations:
        cpus: "1"
        memory: 2G

          cpus: "2"

          cpus: "0.5"
          memory: 1G

    build: *rag-build
    command: ["python", "-m", "services.indexer.main"]
    depends_on:
      - qdrant
    environment:
      QDRANT_URL: "${QDRANT_URL:-http://qdrant:6333}"
      WORKSPACE_ID: "${WORKSPACE_ID:?WORKSPACE_ID must be set — e.g. 'workspace'}"
